redis.description: Redis server found running. If it is exposed without a password, it can lead to remote code execution (RCE) via SSH key writing or cron job manipulation.

mongodb.description: MongoDB server found running. Unauthenticated access can lead to complete database dump and modification. Ensure authentication is enabled.

tomcat.description: Apache Tomcat server found running. Check for default credentials (tomcat:tomcat) on the manager application which can allow WAR file deployment for RCE.

weblogic.description: Oracle WebLogic server found running. Historically affected by numerous critical RCE vulnerabilities via deserialization and console bypass.

microsoft-ds.description: Samba vfs_fruit module allows out-of-bounds heap read and write via extended file attributes, leading to remote code execution as root.
Samba 3.0.20 username map script vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote code execution via shell metacharacters in the username.

mssql.description: Microsoft SQL Server found running. Weak credentials (e.g., sa:sa) can lead to command execution via xp_cmdshell if enabled.

docker.description: Docker daemon API found exposed. Unauthenticated access allows full control over the host via privileged container creation and mounting the root filesystem.

postgresql.description: PostgreSQL database found running. Check for default credentials (postgres:postgres). Authenticated attackers can use COPY TO PROGRAM for code execution.
